CORRECTS PREVIOUS TORNADO REPORT FROM SHERMAN. PATH LENGTH 5.5 MILES, WIDTH 400 YARDS,. MAXIMUM PEAK WINDS 130 MPH. A TORNADO DEVELOPED ONE MILE NORTHWEST OF SHERMAN IN AN OPEN FIELD, THEN CAUSED DAMAGE TO TWO HOUSES AND TWO OUTBUILDINGS BEFORE CROSSING I-55. MORE SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE WAS NOTED IN A SUBDIVISION IMMEDIATELY EAST OF I-55. THERE, NUMEROUS HOUSES HAD SEVERE ROOF AND SIDING DAMAGE, AND SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS AND DETACHED GARAGES WERE SEVERELY DAMAGED. THE TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEAST INTO MORE RURAL AREAS, DAMAGING TWO LARGE BARNS AT A HORSE FARM. MINOR DAMAGE WAS NOTED FARTHER NORTHEAST, WITH SHEET METAL FROM OUTBUILDINGS STREWN INTO OPEN FIELDS. THE TORNADO LIFTED 2 MILES SOUTHEAST OF WILLIAMSVILLE.
TORNADO ON GROUND NEAR COUNTRY MARKET IN SHERMAN.

PATH LENGTH 21 MILES, PEAK WIDTH 200 YARDS, MAX WIND 130 MPH. THE TORNADO DEVELOPED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF OVERPASS RD AND OLD RT. 36 CAUSING DAMAGE TO TREES. NORTHEAST OF THIS LOCATION, THE ROOF WAS COMPLETELY REMOVED FROM A BUSINESS. THE REMAINDER OF THE TORNADO PATH THROUGH NORTHERN SANGAMON AND SOUTHERN LOGAN COUNTIES WAS MAINLY IN OPEN FIELDS, WITH MINOR DAMAGE NOTED TO FARM OUTBUILDINGS. THE TORNADO DISSIPATED NEAR LATHAM, PRODUCING SHINGLE DAMAGE TO SEVERAL HOUSES IN TOWN.
STORM CHASER REPORTING A STOVEPIPE TORNADO TOUCHDOWN N OF TACO JOHNS OUTSIDE OF RIVERTON THAT HIT SEVERAL HOMES. SAW SEVERE DAMAGE ON THE HOMES WHILE TRAVELLING E ON I-72.

*** 3 FATAL, 8 INJ *** PATH LENGTH 27.7 MILES IN ILLINOIS AND ANOTHER 13.15 MILES IN INDIANA. MAX WIDTH IN ILLINOIS 0.4 MILES AND PEAK WIND SPEED 150 MPH. TIME ON THE GROUND IN ILLINOIS WAS FROM 8:58 PM CDT TO 9:21 PM CDT. TOUCHED DOWN SOUTH OF STE. MARIE ALONG COUNTY HIGHWAY 16. Path length 9.16 miles, max width 800 yards, max wind speed 120 mph. Path extended from 1 mile south of Curran to 2 miles north-northeast of Glenarm. Most of the damage path was EF1 intensity damage. The worst damage occurred near the start of the track, at the intersection of Curran Rd. and Spaulding Orchard Rd., where large sections of roof structure were removed from a home.
Large tree down blocking subdivision road.
Tree down on a car and power lines. Time estimated by radar.
At least 15 power poles down. Time estimated by radar.

Corn crops laying over.

Path length 4.42 miles, max width 800 yards, peak wind speed 100 mph. Traveled from the north edge of Lincoln to 2 miles south-southwest of Lawndale. The tornado began near the Eaton plant along Lincoln Parkway on the north side of town, and tracked northeast. Its greatest strength occurred as it downed large electrical poles near exit 133 on Interstate 55. This tornado passed within 2 miles of the National Weather Service office at the Logan County Airport.
Two foot diameter tree blown down. Shingles blown off apartment complex. Possibly caused by tornado. Time estimated by radar.

Sentinel-2 satellite imagery showed that a tornado touched down about half mile north of the intersection of N. 200 East Rd. and E. 1100 North Rd., and generally traveled east-southeast through farm fields, lifting before reaching N. 300 East Rd. Damage appeared limited to crops, thus no intensity could be assigned. Less than a mile beyond the track, evidence of a microburst was seen just north of 1100 North Rd. between 300 East and 420 East Roads.
Numerous tree branches down.

Path length 3.46 miles, max width 200 yards, max wind speed 95 mph. Traveled from 1 mile north of Bulpitt to 1 mile southwest of Sharpsburg. Touched down just east of Sangchris Lake State Park. While most of the damage was to trees, a metal farm building was demolished near the intersection of E. 1800 North Rd. and N. 800 East Rd.
Large tree broken along 1400th Ave, and several tree branches down along Airport Rd.
Tree fell onto residence causing significant damage.
7 to 8 power poles were blown down at I-55 Exit 133 just north of Lincoln. Power lines were draped across the bridge that goes over Route 66.
Grain bins were damaged along US-136.
Many power lines down along Route 121.
Large tree branches down.
A large tree was blown down.
Many power lines down in Lincoln.
Roof blown off of outbuilding.
Large tree branches and one tree trunk broken on 1600th Ave. just north of US-136.
70 year old tree snapped in half and crashed into the roof of a modular home.

Sentinel-2 satellite analysis showed a tornado touched down 1.2 miles south-southwest of Mt. Auburn and moved southeast. Numerous trees and a farm building were damaged along N. 1500 East Rd. just north of its intersection with E. 2700 North Rd., with a car door impaled by a piece of wooden debris. Maximum intensity was estimated at EF1 100 mph at the damaged farmstead.
Delayed SMS report with pictures from public line out of Blue Mound, IL. Images of outbuilding demolished, damage to window of a home, likely totaled vehicle, and multiple fallen trees/limbs.
Tree limbs down.
Large tree limbs down along highway 10 just west of Midland City.
Power lines down on I-55 near mile marker 133, prompting some road closures.
Emergency manager reports trees through houses across northeastern third of Christian County. 2 barns were also destroyed.
Large tree branches down.

Path length 14.2 miles, max width 800 yards, max wind speed 100 mph. This tornado touched down in far eastern Logan County, before crossing the border into De Witt County 2.3 miles southwest of Waynesville. A farm building collapsed near the intersection of Thorps Rd. and South Rd. The damage path continued east paralleling Thorps Rd. before entering Wapella, where the tornado was at its widest. Extensive tree damage occurred in Wapella, with some home damage as well.
A few large tree branches were blown down.
Large branches down, gutters and shingles blown off.
A shed was destroyed and several trees were damaged at the intersection of Thorps Road and North 100 East Road.
Shingle damage and small limbs down.

Path length 10.4 miles, max width 200 yards, max wind speed 100 mph. This tornado touched down north of Taylorville near the intersection of E. 1700 North Rd. and N. 1250 East Rd. This largely caused tree damage, but it did damage a house northeast of Taylorville. A personal weather station at that location measured a wind speed of 101 mph.

Path length 23.6 miles, peak wind speed 125 mph, max width 450 yards. Touched down in southeast Sangamon County before crossing the Christian County border and over Sangchris Lake. The greatest damage was done about 2 miles south of Willeys.

An NWS storm survey found the damage around Lake Mattoon to be caused by downburst winds to approximately 85 mph. One home on the east side of the lake lost half its roof, and a tree on the west side of the lake fell into a house. Extensive tree damage was noted in an area concentrated south of Shelby County Road 975N and Cumberland County Road 1250N.
